name: addcslashes
description: Quote string with slashes in a C style

params.name: string
params.description: The string to be escaped.

params.name: characters
params.description: A list of characters to be escaped. If characters contains characters \n, \r etc., they are converted in C-like style, while other non-alphanumeric characters with ASCII codes lower than 32 and higher than 126 converted to octal representation. When you define a sequence of characters in the characters argument make sure that you know what characters come between the characters that you set as the start and end of the range. Also, if the first character in a range has a higher ASCII value than the second character in the range, no range will be constructed. Only the start, end and period characters will be escaped. Use the ord function to find the ASCII value for a character. Be careful if you choose to escape characters 0, a, b, f, n, r, t and v. They will be converted to \0, \a, \b, \f, \n, \r, \t and \v, all of which are predefined escape sequences in C. Many of these sequences are also defined in other C-derived languages, including PHP, meaning that you may not get the desired result if you use the output of addcslashes() to generate code in those languages with these characters defined in characters.

returnDescription: the escaped string. Example usage:

name: addslashes
description: Quote string with slashes

params.name: string
params.description: The string to be escaped.

returnDescription: the escaped string.

name: addSubscriber
description: Registers a new monitoring event subscriber with the driver. Registered subscribers will be notified of monitoring events through specific methods. Note: If the object is already registered, this function is a no-op.

params.name: subscriber
params.description: A monitoring event subscriber object to register.

name: apache_child_terminate
description: Terminate apache process after this request apache_child_terminate() will register the Apache process executing the current PHP request for termination once execution of PHP code is completed. It may be used to terminate a process after a script with high memory consumption has been run as memory will usually only be freed internally but not given back to the operating system.
returnDescription: TRUE if PHP is running as an Apache 1 module, the Apache version is non-multithreaded, and the child_terminate PHP directive is enabled (disabled by default). If these conditions are not met, FALSE is returned and an error of level E_WARNING is generated.

name: apache_get_modules
description: Get a list of loaded Apache modules
returnDescription: of loaded Apache modules.

name: apache_get_version
description: Fetch the Apache version
returnDescription: the Apache version on success or FALSE on failure.

name: apache_getenv
description: Get an Apache subprocess_env variable Retrieve an Apache environment variable specified by $variable. This function requires Apache 2 otherwise it's undefined.

params.name: variable
params.description: The Apache environment variable.

params.name: walk_to_top
params.description: Whether to get the top-level variable available to all Apache layers.

returnDescription: The value of the Apache environment variable on success, or FALSE on failure.

name: apache_lookup_uri
description: Perform a partial request for the specified URI and return all info about it This performs a partial request for a URI. It goes just far enough to obtain all the important information about the given resource. This function is supported when PHP is installed as an Apache module or by the NSAPI server module in Netscape/iPlanet/SunONE webservers.

params.name: filename
params.description: The filename (URI) that's being requested.

returnDescription: of related URI information.

name: apache_note
description: Get and set apache request notes This function is a wrapper for Apache's table_get and table_set. It edits the table of notes that exists during a request. The table's purpose is to allow Apache modules to communicate. The main use for apache_note() is to pass information from one module to another within the same request.

params.name: note_name
params.description: The name of the note.

params.name: note_value
params.description: The value of the note.

returnDescription: If called with one argument, it returns the current value of note note_name. If called with two arguments, it sets the value of note note_name to note_value and returns the previous value of note note_name. If the note cannot be retrieved, FALSE is returned.

name: apache_request_headers
description: Fetches all HTTP request headers from the current request
returnDescription: An associative array of all the HTTP headers in the current request, or FALSE on failure.

name: apache_reset_timeout
description: Reset the Apache write timer apache_reset_timeout() resets the Apache write timer, which defaults to 300 seconds. With set_time_limit(0); ignore_user_abort(true) and periodic apache_reset_timeout() calls, Apache can theoretically run forever. This function requires Apache 1.
returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: apache_response_headers
description: Fetch all HTTP response headers
returnDescription: An array of all Apache response headers on success or FALSE on failure.

name: apache_setenv
description: Sets the value of the Apache environment variable specified by variable. Note: When setting an Apache environment variable, the corresponding $_SERVER variable is not changed.

params.name: walk_to_top
params.description: Whether to set the top-level variable available to all Apache layers.

returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: apc_add
description: Caches a variable in the data store, only if it's not already stored

params.name: key
params.description: Store the variable using this name. Keys are cache-unique, so attempting to use apc_add() to store data with a key that already exists will not overwrite the existing data, and will instead return FALSE. (This is the only difference between apc_add() and apc_store().)

params.name: var
params.description: The variable to store

params.name: ttl
params.description: Time To Live; store var in the cache for ttl seconds. After the ttl has passed, the stored variable will be expunged from the cache (on the next request). If no ttl is supplied (or if the ttl is 0), the value will persist until it is removed from the cache manually, or otherwise fails to exist in the cache (clear, restart, etc.).

name: apc_bin_dump
description: Returns a binary dump of the given files and user variables from the APC cache A NULL for files or user_vars signals a dump of every entry, while array() will dump nothing.

params.name: files
params.description: The files. Passing in NULL signals a dump of every entry, while passing in array() will dump nothing.

params.name: user_vars
params.description: The user vars. Passing in NULL signals a dump of every entry, while passing in array() will dump nothing.

returnDescription: Returns a binary dump of the given files and user variables from the APC cache, FALSE if APC is not enabled, or NULL if an unknown error is encountered.

name: apc_bin_dumpfile
description: Output a binary dump of the given files and user variables from the APC cache to the named file

params.name: files
params.description: The file names being dumped.

params.name: user_vars
params.description: The user variables being dumped.

params.name: filename
params.description: The filename where the dump is being saved.

params.name: flags
params.description: Flags passed to the filename stream. See the file_put_contents() documentation for details.

params.name: context
params.description: The context passed to the filename stream. See the file_put_contents() documentation for details.

returnDescription: The number of bytes written to the file, otherwise FALSE if APC is not enabled, filename is an invalid file name, filename can't be opened, the file dump can't be completed (e.g., the hard drive is out of disk space), or an unknown error was encountered.

name: apc_bin_load
description: Load the given binary dump into the APC file/user cache

params.name: data
params.description: The binary dump being loaded, likely from apc_bin_dump().

params.name: flags
params.description: Either APC_BIN_VERIFY_CRC32, APC_BIN_VERIFY_MD5, or both.

returnDescription: Returns TRUE if the binary dump data was loaded with success, otherwise FALSE is returned. FALSE is returned if APC is not enabled, or if the data is not a valid APC binary dump (e.g., unexpected size).

name: apc_bin_loadfile
description: Load the given binary dump from the named file into the APC file/user cache

params.name: filename
params.description: The file name containing the dump, likely from apc_bin_dumpfile().

params.name: context
params.description: The files context.

params.name: flags
params.description: Either APC_BIN_VERIFY_CRC32, APC_BIN_VERIFY_MD5, or both.

returnDescription: Returns TRUE on success, otherwise FALSE Reasons it may return FALSE include APC is not enabled, filename is an invalid file name or empty, filename can't be opened, the file dump can't be completed, or if the data is not a valid APC binary dump (e.g., unexpected size).

name: apc_cache_info
description: Retrieves cached information and meta-data from APC's data store

params.name: type
params.description: If cache_type is "user", information about the user cache will be returned. If cache_type is "filehits", information about which files have been served from the bytecode cache for the current request will be returned. This feature must be enabled at compile time using --enable-filehits. If an invalid or no cache_type is specified, information about the system cache (cached files) will be returned.

params.name: limited
params.description: If limited is TRUE, the return value will exclude the individual list of cache entries. This is useful when trying to optimize calls for statistics gathering.

returnDescription: Array of cached data (and meta-data) or FALSE on failure.

name: apc_define_constants
description: Defines a set of constants for retrieval and mass-definition define() is notoriously slow. Since the main benefit of APC is to increase the performance of scripts/applications, this mechanism is provided to streamline the process of mass constant definition. However, this function does not perform as well as anticipated. For a better-performing solution, try the hidef extension from PECL.

params.name: key
params.description: The key serves as the name of the constant set being stored. This key is used to retrieve the stored constants in apc_load_constants().

params.name: constants
params.description: An associative array of constant_name => value pairs. The constant_name must follow the normal constant naming rules. Value must evaluate to a scalar value.

params.name: case_sensitive
params.description: The default behaviour for constants is to be declared case-sensitive; i.e. CONSTANT and Constant represent different values. If this parameter evaluates to FALSE the constants will be declared as case-insensitive symbols.

returnDescription: Returns TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

returnDescription: Returns TRUE on success or FALSE on failure | array with error keys.

description: Caches a variable in the data store, only if it's not already stored

If no ttl is supplied (or if the ttl is 0), the value will persist until it is removed from the cache manually, or otherwise fails to exist in the cache (clear, restart, etc.).

name: apcu_entry
description: Atomically fetch or generate a cache entry Atomically attempts to find key in the cache, if it cannot be found generator is called, passing key as the only argument. The return value of the call is then cached with the optionally specified ttl, and returned. Note: When control enters apcu_entry() the lock for the cache is acquired exclusively, it is released when control leaves apcu_entry(): In effect, this turns the body of generator into a critical section, disallowing two processes from executing the same code paths concurrently. In addition, it prohibits the concurrent execution of any other APCu functions, since they will acquire the same lock.

params.name: key
params.description: Identity of cache entry

params.name: generator
params.description: A callable that accepts key as the only argument and returns the value to cache. Warning The only APCu function that can be called safely by generator is apcu_entry().

params.name: ttl
params.description: [optional] Time To Live; store var in the cache for ttl seconds. After the ttl has passed, the stored variable will be expunged from the cache (on the next request). If no ttl is supplied (or if the ttl is 0), the value will persist until it is removed from the cache manually, or otherwise fails to exist in the cache (clear, restart, etc.).

returnDescription: Returns the cached value

params.description: [optional] If specified, then only keys containing these values are returned.

params.name: strict
params.description: [optional] Determines if strict comparison (===) should be used during the search.

returnDescription: an array of all the keys in input.

returnDescription: an array containing all the elements of arr1 after applying the callback function to each one.

name: array_merge
description: Merges the elements of one or more arrays together (if the input arrays have the same string keys, then the later value for that key will overwrite the previous one; if the arrays contain numeric keys, the later value will be appended) Since 7.4.0 this function can be called without any parameter, and it will return empty array.
returnDescription: the resulting array.

name: array_merge_recursive
description: Merge two or more arrays recursively
returnDescription: An array of values resulted from merging the arguments together.

name: array_multisort
description: Sort multiple or multi-dimensional arrays
returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: array_pad
description: Pad array to the specified length with a value

params.name: array
params.description: Initial array of values to pad.

params.name: length
params.description: New size of the array.

params.name: value
params.description: Value to pad if input is less than pad_size.

returnDescription: a copy of the input padded to size specified by pad_size with value pad_value. If pad_size is positive then the array is padded on the right, if it's negative then on the left. If the absolute value of pad_size is less than or equal to the length of the input then no padding takes place.

name: array_pop
description: Pop the element off the end of array
returnDescription: the last value of array. If array is empty (or is not an array), null will be returned.

name: array_product
description: Calculate the product of values in an array

params.name: array
params.description: The array.

returnDescription: the product as an integer or float.

name: array_push
description: Push elements onto the end of array Since 7.3.0 this function can be called with only one parameter. For earlier versions at least two parameters are required.
returnDescription: the number of elements in the array.

name: array_rand
description: Pick one or more random keys out of an array

params.name: array
params.description: The input array.

params.name: num
params.description: [optional] Specifies how many entries you want to pick.

returnDescription: If you are picking only one entry, array_rand returns the key for a random entry. Otherwise, it returns an array of keys for the random entries. This is done so that you can pick random keys as well as values out of the array.

name: array_reduce
description: Iteratively reduce the array to a single value using a callback function

params.name: array
params.description: The input array.

params.name: callback
params.description: The callback function. Signature is callback ( mixed $carry , mixed $item ) : mixed mixed $carry The return value of the previous iteration; on the first iteration it holds the value of $initial . mixed $item Holds the current iteration value of the $input

params.name: initial
params.description: [optional] If the optional initial is available, it will be used at the beginning of the process, or as a final result in case the array is empty.

returnDescription: the resulting value. If the array is empty and initial is not passed, array_reduce returns null. Example use: array_reduce(['2', '3', '4'], function($ax, $dx) { return $ax . ", {$dx}"; }, '1') // Returns '1, 2, 3, 4' array_reduce(['2', '3', '4'], function($ax, $dx) { return $ax + (int)$dx; }, 1) // Returns 10

name: array_replace
description: array_replace() replaces the values of the first array with the same values from all the following arrays. If a key from the first array exists in the second array, its value will be replaced by the value from the second array. If the key exists in the second array, and not the first, it will be created in the first array. If a key only exists in the first array, it will be left as is. If several arrays are passed for replacement, they will be processed in order, the later arrays overwriting the previous values. array_replace() is not recursive : it will replace values in the first array by whatever type is in the second array.

params.name: array
params.description: The array in which elements are replaced.

returnDescription: or null if an error occurs.

name: array_replace_recursive
description: Replaces elements from passed arrays into the first array recursively

params.name: array
params.description: The array in which elements are replaced.

returnDescription: an array, or null if an error occurs.

name: array_reverse
description: Return an array with elements in reverse order

params.name: array
params.description: The input array.

params.name: preserve_keys
params.description: [optional] If set to true keys are preserved.

returnDescription: the reversed array.

name: array_search
description: Searches the array for a given value and returns the first corresponding key if successful

params.name: needle
params.description: The searched value. If needle is a string, the comparison is done in a case-sensitive manner.

params.name: haystack
params.description: The array.

params.name: strict
params.description: [optional] If the third parameter strict is set to true then the array_search function will also check the types of the needle in the haystack.

returnDescription: the key for needle if it is found in the array, false otherwise. If needle is found in haystack more than once, the first matching key is returned. To return the keys for all matching values, use array_keys with the optional search_value parameter instead.

name: array_shift
description: Shift an element off the beginning of array
returnDescription: the shifted value, or null if array is empty or is not an array.

name: array_slice
description: Extract a slice of the array

params.name: array
params.description: The input array.

params.name: offset
params.description: If offset is non-negative, the sequence will start at that offset in the array. If offset is negative, the sequence will start that far from the end of the array.

params.name: length
params.description: [optional] If length is given and is positive, then the sequence will have that many elements in it. If length is given and is negative then the sequence will stop that many elements from the end of the array. If it is omitted, then the sequence will have everything from offset up until the end of the array.

params.name: preserve_keys
params.description: [optional] Note that array_slice will reorder and reset the array indices by default. You can change this behaviour by setting preserve_keys to true.

returnDescription: the slice.

name: array_splice
description: Remove a portion of the array and replace it with something else

params.name: offset
params.description: If offset is positive then the start of removed portion is at that offset from the beginning of the input array. If offset is negative then it starts that far from the end of the input array.

params.name: length
params.description: [optional] If length is omitted, removes everything from offset to the end of the array. If length is specified and is positive, then that many elements will be removed. If length is specified and is negative then the end of the removed portion will be that many elements from the end of the array. Tip: to remove everything from offset to the end of the array when replacement is also specified, use count($input) for length.

params.name: replacement
params.description: If replacement array is specified, then the removed elements are replaced with elements from this array. If offset and length are such that nothing is removed, then the elements from the replacement array are inserted in the place specified by the offset. Note that keys in replacement array are not preserved. If replacement is just one element it is not necessary to put array() around it, unless the element is an array itself.

returnDescription: the array consisting of the extracted elements.

name: array_sum
description: Calculate the sum of values in an array

params.name: array
params.description: The input array.

returnDescription: the sum of values as an integer or float.

params.description: The first array.

returnDescription: an array containing all the values of array that are not present in any of the other arguments.

name: array_udiff_assoc
description: Computes the difference of arrays with additional index check, compares data by a callback function

params.name: array
params.description: The first array.

returnDescription: returns an array containing all the values from array that are not present in any of the other arguments. Note that the keys are used in the comparison unlike array_diff and array_udiff. The comparison of arrays' data is performed by using an user-supplied callback. In this aspect the behaviour is opposite to the behaviour of array_diff_assoc which uses internal function for comparison.

name: array_udiff_uassoc
description: Computes the difference of arrays with additional index check, compares data and indexes by a callback function

params.name: array
params.description: The first array.

returnDescription: an array containing all the values and keys from array that are not present in any of the other arguments.

name: array_uintersect_assoc
description: Computes the intersection of arrays with additional index check, compares data by a callback function

params.name: array
params.description: The first array.

returnDescription: an array containing all the values of array that are present in all the arguments.

name: array_uintersect_uassoc
description: Computes the intersection of arrays with additional index check, compares data and indexes by separate callback functions

params.name: array
params.description: The first array.

returnDescription: an array containing all the values and keys of array1 that are present in all the arguments.

params.description: The input array.

params.description: [optional] The optional second parameter sort_flags may be used to modify the sorting behavior using these values: Sorting type flags: SORT_REGULAR - compare items normally (don't change types) SORT_NUMERIC - compare items numerically SORT_STRING - compare items as strings SORT_LOCALE_STRING - compare items as strings, based on the current locale

name: array_walk
description: Apply a user function to every member of an array

params.name: callback
params.description: Typically, funcname takes on two parameters. The array parameter's value being the first, and the key/index second. If funcname needs to be working with the actual values of the array, specify the first parameter of funcname as a reference. Then, any changes made to those elements will be made in the original array itself. Users may not change the array itself from the callback function. e.g. Add/delete elements, unset elements, etc. If the array that array_walk is applied to is changed, the behavior of this function is undefined, and unpredictable.

params.name: arg
params.description: [optional] If the optional userdata parameter is supplied, it will be passed as the third parameter to the callback funcname.

name: array_walk_recursive
description: Apply a user function recursively to every member of an array

params.name: callback
params.description: Typically, funcname takes on two parameters. The input parameter's value being the first, and the key/index second. If funcname needs to be working with the actual values of the array, specify the first parameter of funcname as a reference. Then, any changes made to those elements will be made in the original array itself.

params.name: arg

name: chmod
description: Changes file mode

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file.

params.name: permissions
params.description: Note that mode is not automatically assumed to be an octal value, so strings (such as "g+w") will not work properly. To ensure the expected operation, you need to prefix mode with a zero (0): The mode parameter consists of three octal number components specifying access restrictions for the owner, the user group in which the owner is in, and to everybody else in this order. One component can be computed by adding up the needed permissions for that target user base. Number 1 means that you grant execute rights, number 2 means that you make the file writeable, number 4 means that you make the file readable. Add up these numbers to specify needed rights. You can also read more about modes on Unix systems with 'man 1 chmod' and 'man 2 chmod'.

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

description: Check for uppercase character(s)

name: ctype_xdigit

However, you need to specify the exact type of the value as an argument when they are NCHAR, BIT, or BLOB/CLOB). The following bind types are supported: "STRING", "NCHAR", "BIT", "NUMERIC", "NUMBER", "FLOAT", "DOUBLE", "TIME", "DATE", "TIMESTAMP", "OBJECT", "BLOB", "CLOB", "NULL".

name: cubrid_close_request

name: cubrid_col_size

params.name: oid

params.name: attr_name
params.description: Attribute name that you want to read from the instance.

returnDescription: Number of elements, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_column_names
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Get the column names in result

params.name: req_identifier
params.description: Request identifier.

returnDescription: Array of string which containing column names, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_column_types
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Get column types in result

params.name: req_identifier
params.description: Request identifier.

returnDescription: Array of string which containing column types, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_commit
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Commit a transaction

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: Connection identifier.

returnDescription: TRUE, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_connect
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.1) Open a connection to a CUBRID Server

params.name: host
params.description: Host name or IP address of CUBRID CAS server.

params.name: port
params.description: Port number of CUBRID CAS server (BROKER_PORT configured in $CUBRID/conf/cubrid_broker.conf).

params.name: dbname
params.description: Name of database.

params.name: userid
params.description: [optional] User name for the database. Default value is 'PUBLIC'.

params.name: passwd
params.description: [optional] User password. Default value is empty string, i.e. no password is defined.

params.name: new_link
params.description: [optional] If a second call is made to cubrid_connect() with the same arguments, no new link will be established, but instead, the connection identifier of the already opened connection will be returned. The new_link parameter modifies this behavior and makes cubrid_connect() always open a new connection, even if cubrid_connect() was called before with the same parameters.

returnDescription: a CUBRID connection identifier on success or false on failure.

name: cubrid_connect_with_url
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.1) Establish the environment for connecting to CUBRID server.

params.name: conn_url
params.description: A character string that contains server connection information. Syntax: 'CUBRID:>host<:>port<:>dbname<:>username<:>password<:?>params<'. E.g. CUBRID:127.0.0.1:33088:demodb:dba:123456:?autocommit=off&althost=10.34.63.132:33088&rctime=100

params.name: userid
params.description: [optional] User name for the database. Default value is 'PUBLIC'.

params.name: passwd
params.description: [optional] User password. Default value is empty string, i.e. no password is defined.

params.name: new_link
params.description: [optional] If a second call is made to cubrid_connect() with the same arguments, no new link will be established, but instead, the connection identifier of the already opened connection will be returned. The new_link parameter modifies this behavior and makes cubrid_connect() always open a new connection, even if cubrid_connect() was called before with the same parameters.

returnDescription: a CUBRID connection identifier on success or false on failure.

name: cubrid_current_oid
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Get OID of the current cursor location

params.name: req_identifier
params.description: Request identifier.

returnDescription: OID of current cursor location, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_data_seek
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Move the internal row pointer of the CUBRID result

params.name: result
params.description: The result.

params.name: row_number
params.description: The desired row number of the new result pointer.

returnDescription: Returns TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: cubrid_db_name
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.1) Get db name from results of cubrid_list_dbs

params.name: result
params.description: The result pointer from a call to cubrid_list_dbs.

params.name: index
params.description: The index into the result set.

returnDescription: the database name on success, and false on failure. If false is returned, use cubrid_error() to determine the nature of the error.

name: cubrid_db_parameter
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.1) Get result data

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: The CUBRID connection. If the connection identifier is not specified, the last link opened by cubrid_connect() is assumed.

returnDescription: An associative array with CUBRID database parameters; on success. FALSE on failure.

name: cubrid_disconnect
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Close CUBRID connection. Same as cubrid_close().

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: [optional] Connection identifier.

returnDescription: Returns true on success or false on failure.

name: cubrid_drop
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Delete an instance using OID

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: Connection identifier.

params.name: oid
params.description: OID of the instance that you want to delete.

returnDescription: TRUE, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_errno
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.1) Returns the numerical value of the error message from previous CUBRID operation. Same as cubrid_error_code();

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: [optional] The CUBRID connection identifier. If the connection identifier is not specified, the last connection opened by cubrid_connect() is assumed.

returnDescription: the error number from the last CUBRID function, or 0 (zero) if no error occurred.

name: cubrid_error
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.1) Get the error message. Same as cubrid_error_msg();

params.name: connection
params.description: [optional] The CUBRID connection.

returnDescription: Error message that occurred.

name: cubrid_error_code
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Get error code for the most recent function call. Same as cubrid_errno();
returnDescription: Error code of the error that occurred, or 0 (zero) if no error occurred.

name: cubrid_error_code_facility
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Get the facility code of error
returnDescription: Facility code of the error code that occurred: CUBRID_FACILITY_DBMS, CUBRID_FACILITY_CAS, CUBRID_FACILITY_CCI, CUBRID_FACILITY_CLIENT

name: cubrid_error_msg
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Get last error message for the most recent function call. Same as cubrid_error();
returnDescription: Error message that occurred.

name: cubrid_execute
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Execute a prepared SQL statement

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: Connection identifier.

params.name: sql
params.description: SQL to be executed.

params.name: option
params.description: [optional] Query execution option CUBRID_INCLUDE_OID, CUBRID_ASYNC, CUBRID_EXEC_QUERY_ALL.

returnDescription: Request identifier, when process is successful, or FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_fetch
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Fetch the next row from a result set

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

params.name: type
params.description: [optional] Array type of the fetched result CUBRID_NUM, CUBRID_ASSOC, CUBRID_BOTH, CUBRID_OBJECT. If you want to operate the lob object, you can use CUBRID_LOB.

returnDescription: array or object, when process is successful. FALSE, when there are no more rows; NULL, when process is unsuccessful. The result can be received either as an array or as an object, and you can decide which data type to use by setting the type argument. The type variable can be set to one of the following values: CUBRID_NUM : Numerical array (0-based) CUBRID_ASSOC : Associative array CUBRID_BOTH : Numerical & Associative array (default) CUBRID_OBJECT : object that has the attribute name as the column name of query result When type argument is omitted, the result will be received using CUBRID_BOTH option as default. When you want to receive query result in object data type, the column name of the result must obey the naming rules for identifiers in PHP. For example, column name such as "count(*)" cannot be received in object type.

name: cubrid_fetch_array
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Fetch a result row as an associative array, a numeric array, or both

params.name: type
params.description: [optional] The type of array that is to be fetched. It's a constant and can take the following values: CUBRID_ASSOC, CUBRID_NUM, and CUBRID_BOTH.

returnDescription: Returns an array of strings that corresponds to the fetched row, when process is successful. FALSE, when there are no more rows; NULL, when process is unsuccessful. The type of returned array depends on how result_type is defined. By using CUBRID_BOTH (default), you'll get an array with both associative and number indices, and you can decide which data type to use by setting the type argument. The type variable can be set to one of the following values: CUBRID_NUM : Numerical array (0-based) CUBRID_ASSOC : Associative array CUBRID_BOTH : Numerical & Associative array (default)

name: cubrid_fetch_assoc
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Return the associative array that corresponds to the fetched row

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

params.name: type
params.description: [optional] Type can only be CUBRID_LOB, this parameter will be used only when you need to operate the lob object.

returnDescription: Associative array, when process is successful. FALSE, when there are no more rows; NULL, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_fetch_field
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.1) Get column information from a result and return as an object

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

params.name: field_offset
params.description: [optional] The numerical field offset. If the field offset is not specified, the next field that was not yet retrieved by this function is retrieved. The field_offset starts at 0.

returnDescription: an object containing field information. The properties of the object are: name - column name table - name of the table the column belongs to def - default value of the column max_length - maximum length of the column not_null - 1 if the column cannot be NULL primary_key - 1 if the column is a primary key unique_key - 1 if the column is a unique key multiple_key - 1 if the column is a non-unique key numeric - 1 if the column is numeric blob - 1 if the column is a BLOB type - the type of the column unsigned - 1 if the column is unsigned zerofill - 1 if the column is zero-filled

name: cubrid_fetch_lengths
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Return an array with the lengths of the values of each field from the current row

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

returnDescription: A numerical array of lengths on success, or false on failure.

name: cubrid_fetch_object
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Fetch the next row and return it as an object

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

params.name: class_name
params.description: [optional] The name of the class to instantiate. If not specified, a stdClass (stdClass is PHP's generic empty class that's used when casting other types to objects) object is returned.

params.name: params
params.description: [optional] An optional array of parameters to pass to the constructor for class_name objects.

params.name: type
params.description: [optional] Type can only be CUBRID_LOB, this parameter will be used only when you need to operate the lob object.

returnDescription: an object with string properties that correspond to the fetched row, or false if there are no more rows, or NULL, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_fetch_row
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Return a numerical array with the values of the current row

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

params.name: type
params.description: Type can only be CUBRID_LOB, this parameter will be used only when you need to operate the lob object.

returnDescription: A numerical array, when process is successful. FALSE, when there are no more rows; NULL, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_field_flags
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Return a string with the flags of the given field offset

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

params.name: field_offset
params.description: The numerical field offset. The field_offset starts at 0. If field_offset does not exist, an error of level E_WARNING is also issued.

returnDescription: a string of flags associated with the result, or FALSE when invalid field_offset value, or -1 if SQL sentence is not SELECT. The following flags are reported, if your version of CUBRID is current enough to support them: "not_null", "primary_key", "unique_key", "foreign_key", "auto_increment", "shared", "reverse_index", "reverse_unique", and "timestamp".

name: cubrid_field_len
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Get the maximum length of the specified field

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

params.name: field_offset
params.description: The field_offset starts at 0. If field_offset does not exist, an error of level E_WARNING is also issued.

returnDescription: Maximum length, when process is successful. FALSE on failure.

name: cubrid_field_name
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Return the name of the specified field index

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

params.name: field_offset
params.description: The field_offset starts at 0. If field_offset does not exist, an error of level E_WARNING is also issued.

returnDescription: The name of the specified field index on success or false on failure.

name: cubrid_field_seek
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Move the result set cursor to the specified field offset

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

params.name: field_offset
params.description: The numerical field offset. The field_offset starts at 0. If field_offset does not exist, an error of level E_WARNING is also issued.

returnDescription: Returns true on success or false on failure.

name: cubrid_field_table
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Return the name of the table of the specified field

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

params.name: field_offset
params.description: The field_offset starts at 0. If field_offset does not exist, an error of level E_WARNING is also issued.

returnDescription: The name of the table on success, FALSE when field_offset value is invalid, or -1 if SQL sentence is not SELECT.

name: cubrid_field_type
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Return the type of the column corresponding to the given field offset

params.name: result
params.description: result comes from a call to cubrid_execute()

params.name: field_offset
params.description: The field_offset starts at 0. If field_offset does not exist, an error of level E_WARNING is also issued.

returnDescription: On success the returned field type will be one of "int", "real", "string", "blob", and others as detailed in the CUBRID documentation. Otherwise, FALSE when invalid field_offset value, or -1 if SQL sentence is not SELECT.

name: cubrid_free_result
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Free the memory occupied by the result data

params.name: req_identifier
params.description: Request identifier.

returnDescription: Returns true on success or false on failure. Note that it can only free the client fetch buffer now, and if you want to free all the memory, use function cubrid_close_request().

name: cubrid_get
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Get a column using OID

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: Connection identifier.

params.name: oid
params.description: OID of the instance that you want to read. To get the current OID of the request, use cubrid_current_oid() function.

params.name: attr
params.description: [optional] Name of the attribute that you want to read.

returnDescription: Content of the requested attribute, when process is successful; When attr is set with string data type, the result is returned as a string; when attr is set with array data type (0-based numerical array), then the result is returned in associative array. When attr is omitted, then all attributes are received in array form. FALSE when process is unsuccessful or result is NULL (If error occurs to distinguish empty string from NULL, then it prints the warning message. You can check the error by using cubrid_error_code()).

name: cubrid_get_autocommit
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.0) Get auto-commit mode of the connection

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: Connection identifier.

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: Connection identifier.

returnDescription: A string that represents the CUBRID connection charset; on success. FALSE on failure.

name: cubrid_get_class_name
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Get the class name using OID

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: Connection identifier.

params.name: oid
params.description: OID of the instance that you want to check the existence. To get the current OID of the request, use cubrid_current_oid() function.

returnDescription: Class name when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_get_client_info
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Return the client library version
returnDescription: A string that represents the client library version; on success. FALSE on failure.

name: cubrid_get_db_parameter
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Returns the CUBRID database parameters

params.name: conn_identifier

returnDescription: Success: the query timeout value of the current request. Units of msec. Failure: FALSE

name: cubrid_get_server_info
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.0) Return the CUBRID server version
returnDescription: A string that represents the CUBRID server version; on success. FALSE on failure.

name: cubrid_is_instance

name: cubrid_list_dbs

params.description: [optional] The CUBRID connection.

name: cubrid_lob_close

params.name: lob_identifier_array
params.description: LOB identifier array return from cubrid_lob_get().

returnDescription: TRUE, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_lob_export

name: cubrid_lob_get
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.1) cubrid_lob_get() is used to get BLOB/CLOB meta info from CUBRID database. CUBRID gets BLOB/CLOB by executing the SQL statement, and returns all LOBs as a resource array. Be sure that the SQL retrieves only one column and its data type is BLOB or CLOB. Remember to use cubrid_lob_close() to release the LOBs if you don't need it any more.

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: Connection identifier.

params.name: sql
params.description: SQL statement to be executed.

returnDescription: Return an array of LOB resources, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_lob_send

params.description: Connection identifier.

params.name: lob_identifier
params.description: LOB identifier.

returnDescription: TRUE, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_lob_size
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.1) Get BLOB/CLOB data size

params.name: lob_identifier
params.description: LOB identifier.

returnDescription: A string representing LOB data size, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

returnDescription: TRUE, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_lob2_close
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.1) Close BLOB/CLOB data

params.name: lob_identifier
params.description: Lob identifier as a result of cubrid_lob2_new() or get from the result set.

name: cubrid_lob2_export
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.1) Export the lob object to a file.

params.description: Lob identifier as a result of cubrid_lob2_new() or get from the result set.

params.name: file_name
params.description: File name you want to store BLOB/CLOB data. It also supports the path of the file.

returnDescription: TRUE if the process is successful and FALSE for failure.

name: cubrid_lob2_import
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.1) Import the lob object from a file.

params.description: Lob identifier as a result of cubrid_lob2_new() or get from the result set.

params.description: File name you want to import BLOB/CLOB data. It also supports the path of the file.

returnDescription: TRUE if the process is successful and FALSE for failure.

name: cubrid_lob2_new
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.1) Create a lob object.

params.name: conn_identifier

params.name: type
params.description: [optional] It may be "BLOB" or "CLOB", it won't be case-sensitive. The default value is "BLOB".

returnDescription: Lob identifier when it is successful. FALSE on failure.

name: cubrid_lob2_read
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.1) Read from BLOB/CLOB data.

params.name: len
params.description: Length from buffer you want to read from the lob data.

returnDescription: Returns the contents as a string. FALSE when there is no more data. NULL on failure.

name: cubrid_lob2_seek
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.1) Move the cursor of a lob object.

params.name: lob_identifier
params.description: Lob identifier as a result of cubrid_lob2_new() or get from the result set.

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of units you want to move the cursor.

params.name: origin
params.description: [optional] This parameter can be the following values: CUBRID_CURSOR_FIRST: move forward from the first beginning. CUBRID_CURSOR_CURRENT: move forward or backward from the current position. CUBRID_CURSOR_LAST: move backward at the end of LOB object.

returnDescription: TRUE if the process is successful and FALSE for failure.

name: cubrid_lob2_seek64
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.1) Move the cursor of a lob object.

params.name: lob_identifier
params.description: Lob identifier as a result of cubrid_lob2_new() or get from the result set.

params.name: offset
params.description: Number of units you want to move the cursor.

params.name: origin
params.description: [optional] This parameter can be the following values: CUBRID_CURSOR_FIRST: move forward from the first beginning. CUBRID_CURSOR_CURRENT: move forward or backward from the current position. CUBRID_CURSOR_LAST: move backward at the end of LOB object.

returnDescription: TRUE if the process is successful and FALSE for failure.

name: cubrid_lob2_size
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.1) Get a lob object's size.

params.name: lob_identifier
params.description: Lob identifier as a result of cubrid_lob2_new() or get from the result set.

returnDescription: It will return the size of the LOB object as a string when it processes successfully. FALSE on failure.

name: cubrid_lob2_size64
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.1) Get a lob object's size.

params.name: lob_identifier
params.description: Lob identifier as a result of cubrid_lob2_new() or get from the result set.

returnDescription: It will return the size of the LOB object as a string when it processes successfully. FALSE on failure.

name: cubrid_lob2_tell
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.1) Tell the cursor position of the LOB object.

params.name: lob_identifier
params.description: Lob identifier as a result of cubrid_lob2_new() or get from the result set.

returnDescription: It will return the cursor position on the LOB object as a string when it processes successfully. FALSE on failure.

name: cubrid_lob2_tell64
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.4.1) Tell the cursor position of the LOB object.

params.name: lob_identifier
params.description: Lob identifier as a result of cubrid_lob2_new() or get from the result set.

returnDescription: It will return the cursor position on the LOB object as a string when it processes successfully. FALSE on failure.

name: cubrid_lob2_write

params.name: userid
params.description: [optional] User name for the database. Default value is 'PUBLIC'.

params.name: passwd
params.description: [optional] User password. Default value is empty string, i.e. no password is defined.

returnDescription: Connection identifier, when process is successful. FALSE, when process is unsuccessful.

name: cubrid_query
description: (PHP 5, CUBRID >= 8.3.1) Send a CUBRID query

params.name: query
params.description: A SQL query. Data inside the query should be properly escaped.

params.name: conn_identifier
params.description: [optional] The CUBRID connection. If the connection identifier is not specified, the last connection opened by cubrid_connect() is assumed.

returnDescription: For SELECT, SHOW, DESCRIBE, EXPLAIN and other statements returning resultset, cubrid_query() returns a resource on success, or false on error. For other type of SQL statements, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, etc, cubrid_query() returns true on success or false on error. The returned result resource should be passed to cubrid_fetch_array(), and other functions for dealing with result tables, to access the returned data. Use cubrid_num_rows() to find out how many rows were returned for a SELECT statement. Use cubrid_affected_rows() to find out how many rows were affected by a DELETE, INSERT, REPLACE, or UPDATE statement. cubrid_query() will also fail and return false if the user does not have permission to access the table(s) referenced by the query.

name: cubrid_real_escape_string

params.name: field

If the column name has been aliased ('select foo as bar from...'), use the alias instead of the column name. If undefined, the first field is retrieved.

returnDescription: Value of a specific field, on success (NULL if value if null). FALSE on failure.

name: date_create_from_format
description: Alias: {@see DateTime::createFromFormat}

params.name: format
params.description: Format accepted by date() . If format does not contain the character ! then portions of the generated time which are not specified in format will be set to the current system time. If format contains the character !, then portions of the generated time not provided in format, as well as values to the left-hand side of the !, will be set to corresponding values from the Unix epoch. The Unix epoch is 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC.

params.name: datetime

params.description: Procedural style only: A {@see DateTime} object returned by {@see date_create()}. The function modifies this object.

params.name: timestamp
params.description: Unix timestamp representing the date.

returnDescription: {@see DateTime} object for call chaining or FALSE on failure

name: date_timezone_get
description: Alias: {@see DateTime::getTimezone}

params.name: object
params.description: Procedural style only: A {@see DateTime} object returned by {@see date_create()}

returnDescription: Returns a {@see DateTimeZone} object on success or FALSE on failure.

name: date_timezone_set
description: Sets the time zone for the datetime object Alias: {@see DateTime::setTimezone}
returnDescription: Returns the {@see DateTime} object for method chaining or FALSE on failure.

name: datefmt_create
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Create a date formatter

params.name: locale
params.description: Locale to use when formatting or parsing.

name: datefmt_format
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Format the date/time value as a string
returnDescription: The formatted string or, if an error occurred, FALSE .

name: datefmt_format_object
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.5.0, PECL intl >= 3.0.0) Formats an object

params.name: datetime
params.description: An object of type IntlCalendar or DateTime. The timezone information in the object will be used.

params.name: format
params.description: [optional] How to format the date/time. This can either be an {https://secure.php.net/manual/en/language.types.array.php array} with two elements (first the date style, then the time style, these being one of the constants IntlDateFormatter::NONE , IntlDateFormatter::SHORT , IntlDateFormatter::MEDIUM , IntlDateFormatter::LONG , IntlDateFormatter::FULL ), a long with the value of one of these constants (in which case it will be used both for the time and the date) or a {@link https://secure.php.net/manual/en/language.types.string.php} with the format described in {@link http://www.icu-project.org/apiref/icu4c/classSimpleDateFormat.html#details the ICU documentation} documentation. If NULL , the default style will be used.

params.name: locale
params.description: [optional] The locale to use, or NULL to use the default one.

returnDescription: The formatted string or, if an error occurred, FALSE .

name: datefmt_get_calendar

returnDescription: The calendar being used by the formatter.

name: datefmt_get_calendar_object
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 3.0.0) Get copy of formatter's calendar object
returnDescription: A copy of the internal calendar object used by this formatter.

name: datefmt_get_datetype
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Get the datetype used for the IntlDateFormatter
returnDescription: The current date type value of the formatter.

name: datefmt_get_error_code
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Get the error code from last operation
returnDescription: The error code, one of UErrorCode values. Initial value is U_ZERO_ERROR.

name: datefmt_get_error_message
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Get the error text from the last operation.
returnDescription: Description of the last error.

name: datefmt_get_locale
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Get the locale used by formatter
returnDescription: the locale of this formatter or 'false' if error

name: datefmt_get_pattern
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Get the pattern used for the IntlDateFormatter
returnDescription: The pattern string being used to format/parse.

name: datefmt_get_timetype
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Get the timetype used for the IntlDateFormatter
returnDescription: The current date type value of the formatter.

name: datefmt_get_timezone
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.5.0, PECL intl >= 3.0.0) Get formatter's timezone
returnDescription: The associated IntlTimeZone object or FALSE on failure.

name: datefmt_get_timezone_id
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Get the timezone-id used for the IntlDateFormatter
returnDescription: ID string for the time zone used by this formatter.

name: datefmt_is_lenient
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Get the lenient used for the IntlDateFormatter
returnDescription: TRUE if parser is lenient, FALSE if parser is strict. By default the parser is lenient.

name: datefmt_localtime

params.description: string to convert to a time

returnDescription: Localtime compatible array of integers : contains 24 hour clock value in tm_hour field
