params.name: blob_id
params.description: A BLOB id.

returnDescription: a BLOB handle for later use with fbird_blob_get or false on failure.

name: fbird_close
description: This is an alias of ibase_close Close a connection to an InterBase database

params.name: connection_id
params.description: [optional] An InterBase link identifier returned from fbird_connect. If omitted, the last opened link is assumed.

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: fbird_commit
description: This is an alias of ibase_commit Commit a transaction

params.name: link_or_trans_identifier
params.description: [optional] If called without an argument, this function commits the default transaction of the default link. If the argument is a connection identifier, the default transaction of the corresponding connection will be committed. If the argument is a transaction identifier, the corresponding transaction will be committed.

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: fbird_commit_ret
description: This is an alias of ibase_commit_ret Commit a transaction without closing it

params.name: link_or_trans_identifier
params.description: [optional] If called without an argument, this function commits the default transaction of the default link. If the argument is a connection identifier, the default transaction of the corresponding connection will be committed. If the argument is a transaction identifier, the corresponding transaction will be committed. The transaction context will be retained, so statements executed from within this transaction will not be invalidated.

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: fbird_connect
description: This is an alias of ibase_connect Open a connection to an InterBase database

params.name: database
params.description: [optional] The database argument has to be a valid path to database file on the server it resides on. If the server is not local, it must be prefixed with either 'hostname:' (TCP/IP), '//hostname/' (NetBEUI) or 'hostname@' (IPX/SPX), depending on the connection protocol used.

params.name: username
params.description: [optional] The user name. Can be set with the fbird.default_user &php.ini; directive.

params.name: password
params.description: [optional] The password for username. Can be set with the fbird.default_password &php.ini; directive.

params.name: charset
params.description: [optional] charset is the default character set for a database.

params.name: buffers
params.description: [optional] buffers is the number of database buffers to allocate for the server-side cache. If 0 or omitted, server chooses its own default.

params.name: dialect
params.description: [optional] dialect selects the default SQL dialect for any statement executed within a connection, and it defaults to the highest one supported by client libraries. Functional only with InterBase 6 and up.

params.name: role
params.description: [optional] Functional only with InterBase 5 and up.

params.name: sync
params.description: [optional]

returnDescription: an InterBase link identifier on success, or false on error.

name: fbird_db_info
description: This is an alias of ibase_db_info Request statistics about a database

params.name: argument
params.description: [optional]

name: fbird_delete_user
description: This is an alias of ibase_delete_user Delete a user from a security database (only for IB6 or later)
returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: fbird_drop_db
description: This is an alias of ibase_drop_db Drops a database

params.name: connection
params.description: [optional] An InterBase link identifier. If omitted, the last opened link is assumed.

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: fbird_errcode
description: This is an alias of ibase_errcode Return an error code
returnDescription: the error code as an integer, or false if no error occurred.

name: fbird_errmsg
description: This is an alias of ibase_errmsg Return error messages
returnDescription: the error message as a string, or false if no error occurred.

name: fbird_execute
description: This is an alias of ibase_execute Execute a previously prepared query

params.name: query
params.description: An InterBase query prepared by fbird_prepare.

returnDescription: If the query raises an error, returns false. If it is successful and there is a (possibly empty) result set (such as with a SELECT query), returns a result identifier. If the query was successful and there were no results, returns true. In PHP 5.0.0 and up, this function returns the number of rows affected by the query (if > 0 and applicable to the statement type). A query that succeeded, but did not affect any rows (e.g. an UPDATE of a non-existent record) will return true.

name: fbird_fetch_assoc
description: This is an alias of ibase_fetch_assoc Fetch a result row from a query as an associative array

params.name: result
params.description: The result handle.

params.name: fetch_flag
params.description: [optional] fetch_flag is a combination of the constants IBASE_TEXT and IBASE_UNIXTIME ORed together. Passing IBASE_TEXT will cause this function to return BLOB contents instead of BLOB ids. Passing IBASE_UNIXTIME will cause this function to return date/time values as Unix timestamps instead of as formatted strings.

returnDescription: an associative array that corresponds to the fetched row. Subsequent calls will return the next row in the result set, or false if there are no more rows.

name: fbird_fetch_object
description: This is an alias of ibase_fetch_object Get an object from a InterBase database

params.name: result_id
params.description: An InterBase result identifier obtained either by fbird_query or fbird_execute.

params.name: fetch_flag
params.description: [optional] fetch_flag is a combination of the constants IBASE_TEXT and IBASE_UNIXTIME ORed together. Passing IBASE_TEXT will cause this function to return BLOB contents instead of BLOB ids. Passing IBASE_UNIXTIME will cause this function to return date/time values as Unix timestamps instead of as formatted strings.

returnDescription: an object with the next row information, or false if there are no more rows.

name: fbird_fetch_row
description: This is an alias of ibase_fetch_row Fetch a row from an InterBase database

params.name: result_identifier
params.description: An InterBase result identifier.

params.name: fetch_flag
params.description: [optional] fetch_flag is a combination of the constants IBASE_TEXT and IBASE_UNIXTIME ORed together. Passing IBASE_TEXT will cause this function to return BLOB contents instead of BLOB ids. Passing IBASE_UNIXTIME will cause this function to return date/time values as Unix timestamps instead of as formatted strings.

returnDescription: an array that corresponds to the fetched row, or false if there are no more rows. Each result column is stored in an array offset, starting at offset 0.

name: fbird_field_info
description: This is an alias of ibase_field_info Get information about a field

params.name: result
params.description: An InterBase result identifier.

params.name: field_number
params.description: Field offset.

returnDescription: an array with the following keys: name, alias, relation, length and type.

params.name: query

returnDescription: the number of parameters as an integer.

params.name: param_number

returnDescription: an array with the following keys: name, alias, relation, length and type.

name: fbird_pconnect
description: This is an alias of ibase_pconnect Open a persistent connection to an InterBase database

params.name: database
params.description: [optional] The database argument has to be a valid path to database file on the server it resides on. If the server is not local, it must be prefixed with either 'hostname:' (TCP/IP), '//hostname/' (NetBEUI) or 'hostname@' (IPX/SPX), depending on the connection protocol used.

params.name: username
params.description: [optional] The user name. Can be set with the fbird.default_user &php.ini; directive.

params.name: password
params.description: [optional] The password for username. Can be set with the fbird.default_password &php.ini; directive.

params.name: charset
params.description: [optional] charset is the default character set for a database.

params.name: buffers
params.description: [optional] buffers is the number of database buffers to allocate for the server-side cache. If 0 or omitted, server chooses its own default.

params.name: dialect
params.description: [optional] dialect selects the default SQL dialect for any statement executed within a connection, and it defaults to the highest one supported by client libraries. Functional only with InterBase 6 and up.

params.name: role
params.description: [optional] Functional only with InterBase 5 and up.

params.name: sync
params.description: [optional]

returnDescription: an InterBase link identifier on success, or false on error.

name: fbird_prepare
description: This is an alias of ibase_prepare Prepare a query for later binding of parameter placeholders and execution

params.name: query
params.description: An InterBase query.

returnDescription: a prepared query handle, or false on error.

name: fbird_query
description: This is an alias of ibase_query Execute a query on an InterBase database

params.name: link_identifier
params.description: [optional] An InterBase link identifier. If omitted, the last opened link is assumed.

params.name: query
params.description: An InterBase query.

params.name: bind_args
params.description: [optional]

returnDescription: If the query raises an error, returns false. If it is successful and there is a (possibly empty) result set (such as with a SELECT query), returns a result identifier. If the query was successful and there were no results, returns true. In PHP 5.0.0 and up, this function will return the number of rows affected by the query for INSERT, UPDATE and DELETE statements. In order to retain backward compatibility, it will return true for these statements if the query succeeded without affecting any rows.

name: fbird_restore
description: This is an alias of ibase_restore Initiates a restore task in the service manager and returns immediately

params.name: options
params.description: [optional]

params.description: [optional]

name: fbird_rollback
description: This is an alias of ibase_rollback Roll back a transaction

params.name: link_or_trans_identifier
params.description: [optional] If called without an argument, this function rolls back the default transaction of the default link. If the argument is a connection identifier, the default transaction of the corresponding connection will be rolled back. If the argument is a transaction identifier, the corresponding transaction will be rolled back.

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: fbird_rollback_ret
description: This is an alias of ibase_rollback_ret Roll back a transaction without closing it

params.name: link_or_trans_identifier
params.description: [optional] If called without an argument, this function rolls back the default transaction of the default link. If the argument is a connection identifier, the default transaction of the corresponding connection will be rolled back. If the argument is a transaction identifier, the corresponding transaction will be rolled back. The transaction context will be retained, so statements executed from within this transaction will not be invalidated.

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: fbird_set_event_handler
description: This is an alias of ibase_set_event_handler Register a callback function to be called when events are posted

params.name: event_handler
params.description: The callback is called with the event name and the link resource as arguments whenever one of the specified events is posted by the database. The callback must return false if the event handler should be canceled. Any other return value is ignored. This function accepts up to 15 event arguments.

params.name: event_name1
params.description: An event name.

params.name: event_name2
params.description: [optional] At most 15 events allowed.

returnDescription: The return value is an event resource. This resource can be used to free the event handler using fbird_free_event_handler.

name: fbird_trans
description: This is an alias of ibase_trans Begin a transaction

params.name: trans_args
params.description: [optional] trans_args can be a combination of IBASE_READ, IBASE_WRITE, IBASE_COMMITTED, IBASE_CONSISTENCY, IBASE_CONCURRENCY, IBASE_REC_VERSION, IBASE_REC_NO_VERSION, IBASE_WAIT and IBASE_NOWAIT.

params.name: link_identifier
params.description: [optional] An InterBase link identifier. If omitted, the last opened link is assumed.

returnDescription: a transaction handle, or false on error.

name: fbird_wait_event
description: This is an alias of ibase_wait_event Wait for an event to be posted by the database

params.name: event_name1
params.description: The event name.

params.name: event_name2
params.description: [optional]

returnDescription: the name of the event that was posted.

name: fclose
description: Closes an open file pointer

params.name: stream
params.description: The file pointer must be valid, and must point to a file successfully opened by fopen or fsockopen.

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: fdatasync
description: Sync file data only to storage. Similar to fsync but does not flush modified metadata. POSIX only, aliased to fsync on Win32.

name: fdiv
description: Performs a floating-point division under IEEE 754 semantics. Division by zero is considered well-defined and will return one of Inf, -Inf or NaN.

name: feof
description: Tests for end-of-file on a file pointer

params.name: stream
params.description: The file pointer must be valid, and must point to a file successfully opened by fopen() or fsockopen() (and not yet closed by fclose()).

returnDescription: true if the file pointer is at EOF or an error occurs (including socket timeout); otherwise returns false.

name: fflush
description: Flushes the output to a file

params.name: stream
params.description: The file pointer must be valid, and must point to a file successfully opened by fopen() or fsockopen() (and not yet closed by fclose()).

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: fgetc
description: Gets character from file pointer

params.name: stream
params.description: The file pointer must be valid, and must point to a file successfully opened by fopen() or fsockopen() (and not yet closed by fclose()).

returnDescription: a string containing a single character read from the file pointed to by handle. Returns false on EOF.

name: fgetcsv
description: Gets line from file pointer and parse for CSV fields

params.name: stream
params.description: A valid file pointer to a file successfully opened by fopen, popen, or fsockopen.

params.name: length
params.description: Must be greater than the longest line (in characters) to be found in the CSV file (allowing for trailing line-end characters). It became optional in PHP 5. Omitting this parameter (or setting it to 0 in PHP 5.0.4 and later) the maximum line length is not limited, which is slightly slower.

params.name: separator
params.description: [optional] Set the field delimiter (one character only).

params.name: enclosure
params.description: [optional] Set the field enclosure character (one character only).

params.name: escape
params.description: [optional] Set the escape character (one character only). Defaults as a backslash.

returnDescription: an indexed array containing the fields read. A blank line in a CSV file will be returned as an array comprising a single null field, and will not be treated as an error. fgetcsv returns null if an invalid handle is supplied or false on other errors, including end of file.

name: fgets
description: Gets line from file pointer

params.name: stream
params.description: The file pointer must be valid, and must point to a file successfully opened by fopen() or fsockopen() (and not yet closed by fclose()).

params.name: length
params.description: [optional] Reading ends when length - 1 bytes have been read, on a newline (which is included in the return value), or on EOF (whichever comes first). If no length is specified, it will keep reading from the stream until it reaches the end of the line. Until PHP 4.3.0, omitting it would assume 1024 as the line length. If the majority of the lines in the file are all larger than 8KB, it is more resource efficient for your script to specify the maximum line length.

returnDescription: a string of up to length - 1 bytes read from the file pointed to by handle. If an error occurs, returns false.

name: fgetss
description: Gets line from file pointer and strip HTML tags

params.name: handle
params.description: The file pointer must be valid, and must point to a file successfully opened by fopen() or fsockopen() (and not yet closed by fclose()).

params.name: length
params.description: [optional] Length of the data to be retrieved.

params.name: allowable_tags
params.description: [optional] You can use the optional third parameter to specify tags which should not be stripped.

returnDescription: a string of up to length - 1 bytes read from the file pointed to by handle, with all HTML and PHP code stripped. If an error occurs, returns false.

name: file
description: Reads entire file into an array

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file.

params.name: flags
params.description: The optional parameter flags can be one, or more, of the following constants: FILE_USE_INCLUDE_PATH - Search for the file in the include_path. FILE_IGNORE_NEW_LINES - Omit newline at the end of each array element FILE_SKIP_EMPTY_LINES - Skip empty lines

params.name: context
params.description: [optional] A context resource created with the stream_context_create function.

returnDescription: the file in an array. Each element of the array corresponds to a line in the file, with the newline still attached. Upon failure, file returns false. Each line in the resulting array will include the line ending, unless FILE_IGNORE_NEW_LINES is used, so you still need to use rtrim if you do not want the line ending present.

name: file_exists
description: Checks whether a file or directory exists

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file or directory. On windows, use //computername/share/filename or \\computername\share\filename to check files on network shares.

returnDescription: true if the file or directory specified by filename exists; false otherwise. This function will return false for symlinks pointing to non-existing files. This function returns false for files inaccessible due to safe mode restrictions. However these files still can be included if they are located in safe_mode_include_dir. The check is done using the real UID/GID instead of the effective one.

name: file_get_contents
description: Reads entire file into a string

params.name: filename
params.description: Name of the file to read.

params.name: use_include_path
params.description: [optional] Note: As of PHP 5 the FILE_USE_INCLUDE_PATH constant can be used to trigger include path search.

params.name: context
params.description: [optional] A valid context resource created with stream_context_create. If you don't need to use a custom context, you can skip this parameter by null.

params.name: offset
params.description: [optional] The offset where the reading starts.

params.name: length
params.description: [optional] Maximum length of data read. The default is to read until end of file is reached.

returnDescription: The function returns the read data or false on failure.

name: file_put_contents
description: Write a string to a file

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file where to write the data.

params.name: data
params.description: The data to write. Can be either a string, an array or a stream resource. If data is a stream resource, the remaining buffer of that stream will be copied to the specified file. This is similar with using stream_copy_to_stream. You can also specify the data parameter as a single dimension array. This is equivalent to file_put_contents($filename, implode('', $array)).

params.name: flags
params.description: [optional] The value of flags can be any combination of the following flags (with some restrictions), joined with the binary OR (|) operator. Available flags Flag Description FILE_USE_INCLUDE_PATH Search for filename in the include directory. See include_path for more information. FILE_APPEND If file filename already exists, append the data to the file instead of overwriting it. Mutually exclusive with LOCK_EX since appends are atomic and thus there is no reason to lock. LOCK_EX Acquire an exclusive lock on the file while proceeding to the writing. Mutually exclusive with FILE_APPEND.

params.name: context
params.description: [optional] A valid context resource created with stream_context_create.

returnDescription: The function returns the number of bytes that were written to the file, or false on failure.

name: fileatime
description: Gets last access time of file

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file.

returnDescription: the time the file was last accessed, or false on failure. The time is returned as a Unix timestamp.

name: filectime
description: Gets inode change time of file

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file.

returnDescription: the time the file was last changed, or false on failure. The time is returned as a Unix timestamp.

name: filegroup
description: Gets file group

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file.

returnDescription: the group ID of the file, or false in case of an error. The group ID is returned in numerical format, use posix_getgrgid to resolve it to a group name. Upon failure, false is returned.

name: fileinode
description: Gets file inode

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file.

returnDescription: the inode number of the file, or false on failure.

name: filemtime
description: Gets file modification time

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file.

returnDescription: the time the file was last modified, or false on failure. The time is returned as a Unix timestamp, which is suitable for the date function.

name: fileowner
description: Gets file owner

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file.

returnDescription: the user ID of the owner of the file, or false on failure. The user ID is returned in numerical format, use posix_getpwuid to resolve it to a username.

name: fileperms
description: Gets file permissions

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file.

returnDescription: the permissions on the file, or false on failure.

name: filesize
description: Gets file size

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file.

returnDescription: the size of the file in bytes, or false (and generates an error of level E_WARNING) in case of an error.

name: filetype
description: Gets file type

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the file.

returnDescription: the type of the file. Possible values are fifo, char, dir, block, link, file, socket and unknown. Returns false if an error occurs. filetype will also produce an E_NOTICE message if the stat call fails or if the file type is unknown.

name: filter_has_var
description: Checks if variable of specified type exists

params.name: input_type
params.description: One of INPUT_GET , INPUT_POST , INPUT_COOKIE , INPUT_SERVER , or INPUT_ENV .

params.name: var_name
params.description: Name of a variable to check.

returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: filter_id
description: Returns the filter ID belonging to a named filter

params.name: name
params.description: Name of a filter to get.

returnDescription: ID of a filter on success or FALSE if filter doesn't exist.

name: filter_input
description: Gets a specific external variable by name and optionally filters it

params.name: type
params.description: One of INPUT_GET , INPUT_POST , INPUT_COOKIE , INPUT_SERVER , or INPUT_ENV .

params.name: var_name
params.description: Name of a variable to get.

params.name: filter
params.description: [optional] The ID of the filter to apply. The manual page lists the available filters.

params.name: options
params.description: Associative array of options or bitwise disjunction of flags. If filter accepts options, flags can be provided in "flags" field of array.

returnDescription: Value of the requested variable on success, FALSE if the filter fails, or NULL if the variable_name variable is not set. If the flag FILTER_NULL_ON_FAILURE is used, it returns FALSE if the variable is not set and NULL if the filter fails.

name: filter_input_array
description: Gets external variables and optionally filters them

params.name: type
params.description: One of INPUT_GET , INPUT_POST , INPUT_COOKIE , INPUT_SERVER , or INPUT_ENV .

params.name: options
params.description: [optional] An array defining the arguments. A valid key is a string containing a variable name and a valid value is either a filter type, or an array optionally specifying the filter, flags and options. If the value is an array, valid keys are filter which specifies the filter type, flags which specifies any flags that apply to the filter, and options which specifies any options that apply to the filter. See the example below for a better understanding. This parameter can be also an integer holding a filter constant. Then all values in the input array are filtered by this filter.

params.name: add_empty
params.description: [optional] Add missing keys as NULL to the return value.

returnDescription: An array containing the values of the requested variables on success, or FALSE on failure. An array value will be FALSE if the filter fails, or NULL if the variable is not set. Or if the flag FILTER_NULL_ON_FAILURE is used, it returns FALSE if the variable is not set and NULL if the filter fails.

name: filter_list
description: Returns a list of all supported filters
returnDescription: an array of names of all supported filters, empty array if there are no such filters. Indexes of this array are not filter IDs, they can be obtained with filter_id from a name instead.

name: filter_var
description: Filters a variable with a specified filter

params.name: value
params.description: Value to filter.

params.name: filter
params.description: [optional] The ID of the filter to apply. The manual page lists the available filters.

params.name: options
params.description: Associative array of options or bitwise disjunction of flags. If filter accepts options, flags can be provided in "flags" field of array. For the "callback" filter, callable type should be passed. The callback must accept one argument, the value to be filtered, and return the value after filtering/sanitizing it. // for filters that accept options, use this format $options = array( 'options' => array( 'default' => 3, // value to return if the filter fails // other options here 'min_range' => 0 ), 'flags' => FILTER_FLAG_ALLOW_OCTAL, ); $var = filter_var('0755', FILTER_VALIDATE_INT, $options); // for filter that only accept flags, you can pass them directly $var = filter_var('oops', FILTER_VALIDATE_BOOLEAN, FILTER_NULL_ON_FAILURE); // for filter that only accept flags, you can also pass as an array $var = filter_var('oops', FILTER_VALIDATE_BOOLEAN, array('flags' => FILTER_NULL_ON_FAILURE)); // callback validate filter function foo($value) { // Expected format: Surname, GivenNames if (strpos($value, ", ") === false) return false; list($surname, $givennames) = explode(", ", $value, 2); $empty = (empty($surname) || empty($givennames)); $notstrings = (!is_string($surname) || !is_string($givennames)); if ($empty || $notstrings) { return false; } else { return $value; } } $var = filter_var('Doe, Jane Sue', FILTER_CALLBACK, array('options' => 'foo'));

returnDescription: the filtered data, or FALSE if the filter fails.

name: filter_var_array
description: Gets multiple variables and optionally filters them

params.name: array
params.description: An array with string keys containing the data to filter.

params.name: options
params.description: [optional] An array defining the arguments. A valid key is a string containing a variable name and a valid value is either a filter type, or an array optionally specifying the filter, flags and options. If the value is an array, valid keys are filter which specifies the filter type, flags which specifies any flags that apply to the filter, and options which specifies any options that apply to the filter. See the example below for a better understanding. This parameter can be also an integer holding a filter constant. Then all values in the input array are filtered by this filter.

params.name: add_empty
params.description: [optional] Add missing keys as NULL to the return value.

returnDescription: An array containing the values of the requested variables on success, or FALSE on failure. An array value will be FALSE if the filter fails, or NULL if the variable is not set.

name: find_active_exception
description: Searches parent frames to see whether it's currently within a catch block and returns that exception.
returnDescription: The active exception if there is one, else 'null'.

name: finfo_buffer
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL fileinfo >= 0.1.0) Return information about a string buffer
returnDescription: a textual description of the string argument, or FALSE if an error occurred.

name: finfo_close

returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: finfo_file

returnDescription: a textual description of the contents of the filename argument, or FALSE if an error occurred.

name: finfo_open
description: (PHP >= 5.3.0, PECL fileinfo >= 0.1.0) Create a new fileinfo resource

params.name: flags
params.description: One or disjunction of more Fileinfo constants.

params.name: magic_database
params.description: [optional] Name of a magic database file, usually something like /path/to/magic.mime. If not specified, the MAGIC environment variable is used. If this variable is not set either, /usr/share/misc/magic is used by default. A .mime and/or .mgc suffix is added if needed.

returnDescription: a magic database resource on success or FALSE on failure.

name: finfo_set_flags
description: (PHP >= 5.3.0, PECL fileinfo >= 0.1.0) Set libmagic configuration options
returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: floatval
description: Get float value of a variable

params.name: value
params.description: May be any scalar type. should not be used on objects, as doing so will emit an E_NOTICE level error and return 1.

returnDescription: value of the given variable. Empty arrays return 0, non-empty arrays return 1.

name: flock
description: Portable advisory file locking

params.name: stream
params.description: An open file pointer.

params.name: operation
params.description: operation is one of the following: LOCK_SH to acquire a shared lock (reader).

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: floor
description: Round fractions down

params.name: num
params.description: The numeric value to round

returnDescription: value rounded to the next lowest integer. The return value of floor is still of type float because the value range of float is usually bigger than that of integer.

name: flush
description: Closes all spans and force-send finished traces to the agent

name: fmod
description: Returns the floating point remainder (modulo) of the division of the arguments

params.name: num1
params.description: The dividend

params.name: num2
params.description: The divisor

returnDescription: The floating point remainder of x/y

name: fnmatch
description: Match filename against a pattern

params.name: pattern
params.description: The shell wildcard pattern.

params.name: filename
params.description: The tested string. This function is especially useful for filenames, but may also be used on regular strings. The average user may be used to shell patterns or at least in their simplest form to '?' and '*' wildcards so using fnmatch instead of preg_match for frontend search expression input may be way more convenient for non-programming users.

params.name: flags
params.description: The value of flags can be any combination of the following flags, joined with the binary OR (|) operator. A list of possible flags for fnmatch Flag Description FNM_NOESCAPE Disable backslash escaping. FNM_PATHNAME Slash in string only matches slash in the given pattern. FNM_PERIOD Leading period in string must be exactly matched by period in the given pattern. FNM_CASEFOLD Caseless match. Part of the GNU extension.

returnDescription: true if there is a match, false otherwise.

name: fopen
description: Opens file or URL

params.name: filename
params.description: If filename is of the form "scheme://...", it is assumed to be a URL and PHP will search for a protocol handler (also known as a wrapper) for that scheme. If no wrappers for that protocol are registered, PHP will emit a notice to help you track potential problems in your script and then continue as though filename specifies a regular file. If PHP has decided that filename specifies a local file, then it will try to open a stream on that file. The file must be accessible to PHP, so you need to ensure that the file access permissions allow this access. If you have enabled "safemode", or open_basedir further restrictions may apply. If PHP has decided that filename specifies a registered protocol, and that protocol is registered as a network URL, PHP will check to make sure that allow_url_fopen is enabled. If it is switched off, PHP will emit a warning and the fopen call will fail. The list of supported protocols can be found in . Some protocols (also referred to as wrappers) support context and/or "php.ini" options. Refer to the specific page for the protocol in use for a list of options which can be set. (e.g. "php.ini" value user_agent used by the http wrapper). On the Windows platform, be careful to escape any backslashes used in the path to the file, or use forward slashes.

params.name: mode
params.description:
The mode parameter specifies the type of access you require to the stream. It may be any of the following: A list of possible modes for fopen using mode mode Description 'r' Open for reading only; place the file pointer at the beginning of the file. 'r+' Open for reading and writing; place the file pointer at the beginning of the file. 'w' Open for writing only; place the file pointer at the beginning of the file and truncate the file to zero length. If the file does not exist, attempt to create it. 'w+' Open for reading and writing; place the file pointer at the beginning of the file and truncate the file to zero length. If the file does not exist, attempt to create it. 'a' Open for writing only; place the file pointer at the end of the file. If the file does not exist, attempt to create it. 'a+' Open for reading and writing; place the file pointer at the end of the file. If the file does not exist, attempt to create it. 'x' Create and open for writing only; place the file pointer at the beginning of the file. If the file already exists, the fopen call will fail by returning false and generating an error of level E_WARNING. If the file does not exist, attempt to create it. This is equivalent to specifying O_EXCL|O_CREAT flags for the underlying open(2) system call. 'x+' Create and open for reading and writing; place the file pointer at the beginning of the file. If the file already exists, the fopen call will fail by returning false and generating an error of level E_WARNING. If the file does not exist, attempt to create it. This is equivalent to specifying O_EXCL|O_CREAT flags for the underlying open(2) system call. Different operating system families have different line-ending conventions. When you write a text file and want to insert a line break, you need to use the correct line-ending character(s) for your operating system. Unix based systems use 
 as the line ending character, Windows based systems use 
 as the line ending characters and Macintosh based systems use  as the line ending character. If you use the wrong line ending characters when writing your files, you might find that other applications that open those files will "look funny". Windows offers a text-mode translation flag ('t') which will transparently translate 
 to 
 when working with the file. In contrast, you can also use 'b' to force binary mode, which will not translate your data. To use these flags, specify either 'b' or 't' as the last character of the mode parameter. The default translation mode depends on the SAPI and version of PHP that you are using, so you are encouraged to always specify the appropriate flag for portability reasons. You should use the 't' mode if you are working with plain-text files and you use 
 to delimit your line endings in your script, but expect your files to be readable with applications such as notepad. You should use the 'b' in all other cases. If you do not specify the 'b' flag when working with binary files, you may experience strange problems with your data, including broken image files and strange problems with 
 characters. For portability, it is strongly recommended that you always use the 'b' flag when opening files with fopen. Again, for portability, it is also strongly recommended that you re-write code that uses or relies upon the 't' mode so that it uses the correct line endings and 'b' mode instead.

params.name: use_include_path
params.description: [optional] The optional third use_include_path parameter can be set to '1' or true if you want to search for the file in the include_path, too.

params.name: context
params.description: [optional]

returnDescription: a file pointer resource on success, or false on error.

name: forward_static_call

params.name: callback
params.description: The function or method to be called. This parameter may be an array, with the name of the class, and the method, or a string, with a function name.

returnDescription: the function result, or false on error.

name: forward_static_call_array
description: Call a static method and pass the arguments as array

params.name: callback
params.description: The function or method to be called. This parameter may be an array, with the name of the class, and the method, or a string, with a function name.

returnDescription: the function result, or false on error.

name: fpassthru
description: Output all remaining data on a file pointer

params.name: stream
params.description: The file pointer must be valid, and must point to a file successfully opened by fopen() or fsockopen() (and not yet closed by fclose()).

returnDescription: If an error occurs, fpassthru returns false. Otherwise, fpassthru returns the number of characters read from handle and passed through to the output.

name: fpm_get_status
description: Returns FPM status info array

name: fpow
description: PHP function fpow

name: fprintf
description: Write a formatted string to a stream

params.name: stream
params.description: &fs.file.pointer;

params.name: format
params.description: See sprintf for a description of format.

returnDescription: the length of the string written.

name: fputcsv
description: Format line as CSV and write to file pointer

params.name: stream
params.description: The file pointer must be valid, and must point to a file successfully opened by fopen() or fsockopen() (and not yet closed by fclose()).

params.name: fields
params.description: An array of values.

params.name: separator
params.description: [optional] The optional delimiter parameter sets the field delimiter (one character only).

params.name: enclosure
params.description: [optional] The optional enclosure parameter sets the field enclosure (one character only).

returnDescription: the length of the written string or false on failure.

name: fputs
description: Alias: {@see fwrite}

params.name: stream
params.description: A file system pointer resource that is typically created using fopen().

params.name: data
params.description: The string that is to be written.

params.name: length
params.description: [optional] If the length argument is given, writing will stop after length bytes have been written or the end of string is reached, whichever comes first. Note that if the length argument is given, then the magic_quotes_runtime configuration option will be ignored and no slashes will be stripped from string.

returnDescription: the number of bytes written, or FALSE on error.

name: frankenphp_finish_request
description: Flushes all response data to the client and finishes the request. This allows for time-consuming tasks to be performed without leaving the connection to the client open. Alias of fastcgi_finish_request .
returnDescription: Returns TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: frankenphp_handle_request
description: Handles an HTTP request with the provided callbacl. When called, superglobals, php://input and the like are reset to reflect the values of the handled request.
returnDescription: returns FALSE if the server is terminating, giving the opportunity to the worker script to finish cleanly

name: frankenphp_log
description: Emits a structured log record with the current time and the given message, level and context.

params.name: level
params.description: The importance or severity of a log event. The higher the level, the more important or severe the event. For more details, see: https://pkg.go.dev/log/slog#Level array $context Values of the array will be converted to the corresponding Go type (if supported by FrankenPHP) and added to the context of the structured logs using https://pkg.go.dev/log/slog#Attr

name: frankenphp_request_headers
description: Fetches all HTTP request headers from the current request. Alias of apache_request_headers .
returnDescription: An associative array of all the HTTP headers in the current request.

name: frankenphp_response_headers
description: Fetches all HTTP response headers. Alias of apache_response_headers .
returnDescription: An array of all FrankenPHP response headers on success or FALSE on failure.

name: fread
description: Binary-safe file read

params.name: stream
params.description: &fs.file.pointer;

params.name: length
params.description: Up to length number of bytes read.

returnDescription: the read string or false on failure.

name: frenchtojd
description: Converts a date from the French Republican Calendar to a Julian Day Count

params.name: month
params.description: The month as a number from 1 (for Vendémiaire) to 13 (for the period of 5-6 days at the end of each year)

params.name: day
params.description: The day as a number from 1 to 30

params.name: year
params.description: The year as a number between 1 and 14

returnDescription: The julian day for the given french revolution date as an integer.

name: fscanf
description: Parses input from a file according to a format

params.name: stream
params.description: &fs.file.pointer;

params.name: format
params.description: The specified format as described in the sprintf documentation.

returnDescription: If only two parameters were passed to this function, the values parsed will be returned as an array. Otherwise, if optional parameters are passed, the function will return the number of assigned values. The optional parameters must be passed by reference.

name: fseek
description: Seeks on a file pointer

params.name: stream
params.description: &fs.file.pointer;

params.name: offset
params.description: The offset. To move to a position before the end-of-file, you need to pass a negative value in offset and set whence to SEEK_END.

params.name: whence
params.description: [optional] whence values are: SEEK_SET - Set position equal to offset bytes. SEEK_CUR - Set position to current location plus offset. SEEK_END - Set position to end-of-file plus offset. If whence is not specified, it is assumed to be SEEK_SET.

returnDescription: Upon success, returns 0; otherwise, returns -1. Note that seeking past EOF is not considered an error.

name: fsockopen
description: Open Internet or Unix domain socket connection

params.name: hostname
params.description: If you have compiled in OpenSSL support, you may prefix the hostname with either ssl:// or tls:// to use an SSL or TLS client connection over TCP/IP to connect to the remote host.

returnDescription: fsockopen returns a file pointer which may be used together with the other file functions (such as fgets, fgetss, fwrite, fclose, and feof). If the call fails, it will return false

name: fstat
description: Gets information about a file using an open file pointer

params.name: stream
params.description: &fs.file.pointer;

returnDescription: an array with the statistics of the file; the format of the array is described in detail on the stat manual page.

name: fsync
description: Sync file to storage. Similar to fflush() but blocks until OS buffers have flushed.

name: ftell
description: Returns the current position of the file read/write pointer

params.name: stream
params.description: The file pointer must be valid, and must point to a file successfully opened by fopen or popen. ftell gives undefined results for append-only streams (opened with "a" flag).

returnDescription: the position of the file pointer referenced by handle as an integer; i.e., its offset into the file stream. If an error occurs, returns false.

name: ftok

params.description: Path to an accessible file.

params.name: project_id
params.description: Project identifier. This must be a one character string.

returnDescription: On success the return value will be the created key value, otherwise -1 is returned.

name: ftp_alloc

name: ftp_append

name: ftp_cdup

name: ftp_chdir

name: ftp_chmod

name: ftp_close

name: ftp_connect

params.name: hostname

params.name: port
params.description: [optional] This parameter specifies an alternate port to connect to. If it is omitted or set to zero, then the default FTP port, 21, will be used.

params.name: timeout
params.description: [optional] This parameter specifies the timeout for all subsequent network operations. If omitted, the default value is 90 seconds. The timeout can be changed and queried at any time with ftp_set_option and ftp_get_option .

returnDescription: a FTP stream on success or FALSE on error.

name: ftp_delete
description: Deletes a file on the FTP server
returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: ftp_exec

returnDescription: TRUE if the command was successful (server sent response code: 200); otherwise returns FALSE .

name: ftp_fget

returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: ftp_fput

returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: ftp_get
description: Downloads a file from the FTP server
returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: ftp_get_option

returnDescription: the value on success or FALSE if the given option is not supported. In the latter case, a warning message is also thrown.

name: ftp_login

returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure. If login fails, PHP will also throw a warning.

name: ftp_mdtm

name: ftp_mkdir

name: ftp_mlsd

name: ftp_nb_fget

returnDescription: FTP_FAILED or FTP_FINISHED or FTP_MOREDATA .

name: ftp_nb_fput

returnDescription: FTP_FAILED or FTP_FINISHED or FTP_MOREDATA .

name: ftp_nb_get

name: ftp_nb_put

name: ftp_pasv

name: ftp_put

returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: ftp_pwd

name: ftp_quit

name: ftp_raw

name: ftp_rawlist

name: ftp_site
description: Sends a SITE command to the server
returnDescription: TRUE on success or FALSE on failure.

name: ftp_size
description: Returns the size of the given file
returnDescription: the file size on success, or -1 on error.

name: ftp_ssl_connect
description: Opens a Secure SSL-FTP connection

params.name: hostname
params.description: The FTP server address. This parameter shouldn't have any trailing slashes and shouldn't be prefixed with ftp://.

params.name: port
params.description: [optional] This parameter specifies an alternate port to connect to. If it is omitted or set to zero, then the default FTP port, 21, will be used.

params.name: timeout
params.description: [optional] This parameter specifies the timeout for all subsequent network operations. If omitted, the default value is 90 seconds. The timeout can be changed and queried at any time with ftp_set_option and ftp_get_option .

returnDescription: a SSL-FTP stream on success or FALSE on error.

name: ftp_systype
description: Returns the system type identifier of the remote FTP server
returnDescription: the remote system type, or FALSE on error.

name: ftruncate
description: Truncates a file to a given length

params.name: stream
params.description: The file pointer. The handle must be open for writing.

params.name: size
params.description: The size to truncate to. If size is larger than the file it is extended with null bytes. If size is smaller than the extra data will be lost.

returnDescription: true on success or false on failure.

name: func_get_arg
description: Return an item from the argument list

params.name: position
params.description: The argument offset. Function arguments are counted starting from zero.

returnDescription: the specified argument, or false on error.

name: func_get_args
description: Returns an array comprising a function's argument list
returnDescription: an array in which each element is a copy of the corresponding member of the current user-defined function's argument list.

name: func_num_args
description: Returns the number of arguments passed to the function
returnDescription: the number of arguments passed into the current user-defined function.

name: function_exists
description: Return true if the given function has been defined

params.name: function
params.description: The function name, as a string.

returnDescription: true if function_name exists and is a function, false otherwise. This function will return false for constructs, such as include_once and echo .

name: fwrite
description: Binary-safe file write

params.name: stream
params.description: &fs.file.pointer;

params.name: data
params.description: The string that is to be written.

params.name: length
params.description: [optional] If the length argument is given, writing will stop after length bytes have been written or the end of string is reached, whichever comes first. Note that if the length argument is given, then the magic_quotes_runtime configuration option will be ignored and no slashes will be stripped from string.

returnDescription: the number of bytes written, or FALSE on error.

name: gc_collect_cycles
description: Forces collection of any existing garbage cycles
returnDescription: number of collected cycles.

name: gc_disable
description: Deactivates the circular reference collector

name: gc_enable
description: Activates the circular reference collector

name: gc_enabled
description: Returns status of the circular reference collector
returnDescription: true if the garbage collector is enabled, false otherwise.

name: gc_mem_caches
description: Reclaims memory used by the Zend Engine memory manager
returnDescription: Returns the number of bytes freed.

name: gc_status
description: Gets information about the garbage collector
returnDescription: associative array with the following elements: "runs" "collected" "threshold" "roots"

description: Get information on the key-value pairs of the datadog headers for distributed tracing

params.description: The types of sampling headers which shall be generated, equivalent to what DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_INJECT supports. Defaults to DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_INJECT if null.

returnDescription: string, x-datadog-origin: string, x-datadog-trace-id: string, x-datadog-parent-id: string, traceparent: string, tracestate: string }

name: geoip_asnum_by_name
description: (PECL geoip >= 1.1.0) The geoip_asnum_by_name() function will return the Autonomous System Numbers (ASN) associated with an IP address.

params.name: hostname
params.description: The hostname or IP address

returnDescription: Returns the ASN on success, or FALSE if the address cannot be found in the database.

name: geoip_continent_code_by_name
description: (PECL geoip >= 1.0.3) Get the two letter continent code

params.name: hostname
params.description: The hostname or IP address whose location is to be looked-up.

returnDescription: the two letter continent code on success, or FALSE if the address cannot be found in the database.

name: geoip_country_code_by_name
description: (PECL geoip >= 0.2.0) Get the two letter country code

params.name: hostname
params.description: The hostname or IP address whose location is to be looked-up.

returnDescription: the two letter ISO country code on success, or FALSE if the address cannot be found in the database.

name: geoip_country_code3_by_name
description: (PECL geoip >= 0.2.0) Get the three letter country code

params.name: hostname
params.description: The hostname or IP address whose location is to be looked-up.

returnDescription: the three letter country code on success, or FALSE if the address cannot be found in the database.

name: geoip_country_name_by_name
description: (PECL geoip >= 0.2.0) Get the full country name

params.name: hostname
params.description: The hostname or IP address whose location is to be looked-up.

returnDescription: the country name on success, or FALSE if the address cannot be found in the database.

name: geoip_database_info
description: (PECL geoip >= 0.2.0) Get GeoIP Database information

params.name: database
params.description: [optional] The database type as an integer. You can use the various constants defined with this extension (ie: GEOIP_*_EDITION).

returnDescription: the corresponding database version, or NULL on error.

name: geoip_db_avail
description: (PECL geoip >= 1.0.1) Determine if GeoIP Database is available

params.name: database
params.description: The database type as an integer. You can use the various constants defined with this extension (ie: GEOIP_*_EDITION).

returnDescription: TRUE is database exists, FALSE if not found, or NULL on error.

name: geoip_db_filename
description: (PECL geoip >= 1.0.1) Returns the filename of the corresponding GeoIP Database

params.name: database
params.description: The database type as an integer. You can use the various constants defined with this extension (ie: GEOIP_*_EDITION).

returnDescription: the filename of the corresponding database, or NULL on error.

name: geoip_db_get_all_info
description: (PECL geoip >= 1.0.1) Returns detailed information about all GeoIP database types
returnDescription: the associative array.

name: geoip_id_by_name
description: (PECL geoip >= 0.2.0) Get the Internet connection type

params.name: hostname
params.description: The hostname or IP address whose connection type is to be looked-up.

returnDescription: the connection type.

name: geoip_isp_by_name
description: (PECL geoip >= 1.0.2) Get the Internet Service Provider (ISP) name

params.name: hostname
params.description: The hostname or IP address.

returnDescription: the ISP name on success, or FALSE if the address cannot be found in the database.

name: geoip_netspeedcell_by_name
description: (PECL geoip >= 1.1.0) The geoip_netspeedcell_by_name() function will return the Internet connection type and speed corresponding to a hostname or an IP address. This function is only available if using GeoIP Library version 1.4.8 or newer. This function is currently only available to users who have bought a commercial GeoIP NetSpeedCell Edition. A warning will be issued if the proper database cannot be located. The return value is a string, common values are: - Cable/DSL - Dialup - Cellular - Corporate

params.name: hostname
params.description: The hostname or IP address

returnDescription: Returns the connection speed on success, or FALSE if the address cannot be found in the database.

name: geoip_org_by_name
description: (PECL geoip >= 0.2.0) Get the organization name

params.name: hostname
params.description: The hostname or IP address.

returnDescription: the organization name on success, or FALSE if the address cannot be found in the database.

name: geoip_record_by_name
description: (PECL geoip >= 0.2.0) Returns the detailed City information found in the GeoIP Database

params.name: hostname
params.description: The hostname or IP address whose record is to be looked-up.

returnDescription: the associative array on success, or FALSE if the address cannot be found in the database.

name: geoip_region_by_name
description: (PECL geoip >= 0.2.0) Get the country code and region

params.name: hostname
params.description: The hostname or IP address whose region is to be looked-up.

returnDescription: the associative array on success, or FALSE if the address cannot be found in the database.

name: geoip_region_name_by_code
description: (PECL geoip >= 1.0.4) Returns the region name for some country and region code combo

params.name: country_code
params.description: The two-letter country code (see geoip_country_code_by_name )

params.name: region_code
params.description: The two-letter (or digit) region code (see geoip_region_by_name )

returnDescription: the region name on success, or FALSE if the country and region code combo cannot be found.

name: geoip_setup_custom_directory
description: (PECL geoip >= 1.1.0) The geoip_setup_custom_directory() function will change the default directory of the GeoIP database. This is equivalent to changing geoip.custom_directory

params.name: path
params.description: The full path of where the GeoIP database is on disk.

name: geoip_time_zone_by_country_and_region
description: (PECL geoip >= 1.0.4) Returns the time zone for some country and region code combo

params.name: country_code
params.description: The two-letter country code (see geoip_country_code_by_name )

params.name: region_code
params.description: [optional] The two-letter (or digit) region code (see geoip_region_by_name )

returnDescription: the time zone on success, or FALSE if the country and region code combo cannot be found.

description: PHP function GEOSPolygonize
returnDescription: - 'rings' Type: array of GEOSGeometry Rings that can be formed by the costituent linework of geometry. - 'cut_edges' (optional) Type: array of GEOSGeometry Edges which are connected at both ends but which do not form part of polygon. - 'dangles' Type: array of GEOSGeometry Edges which have one or both ends which are not incident on another edge endpoint - 'invalid_rings' Type: array of GEOSGeometry Edges which form rings which are invalid (e.g. the component lines contain a self-intersection)

name: get_browser
description: Tells what the user's browser is capable of

params.name: user_agent
params.description: [optional] The User Agent to be analyzed. By default, the value of HTTP User-Agent header is used; however, you can alter this (i.e., look up another browser's info) by passing this parameter. You can bypass this parameter with a null value.

params.name: return_array
params.description: [optional] If set to true, this function will return an array instead of an object.

returnDescription: Returns false if browscap.ini can't be loaded or the user agent can't be found, otherwise the information is returned in an object or an array which will contain various data elements representing, for instance, the browser's major and minor version numbers and ID string; true/false values for features such as frames, JavaScript, and cookies; and so forth. The cookies value simply means that the browser itself is capable of accepting cookies and does not mean the user has enabled the browser to accept cookies or not. The only way to test if cookies are accepted is to set one with setcookie, reload, and check for the value.

name: get_call_stack
description: PHP function get_call_stack

name: get_called_class
description: the "Late Static Binding" class name

name: get_cfg_var
description: Gets the value of a PHP configuration option

params.name: option
params.description: The configuration option name.

returnDescription: the current value of the PHP configuration variable specified by option, or false if an error occurs.

name: get_class
description: Returns the name of the class of an object

params.name: object
params.description: [optional] The tested object. This parameter may be omitted when inside a class.

returnDescription: The name of the class of which object is an instance. If object is omitted when inside a class, the name of that class is returned.

name: get_class_methods
description: Gets the class methods' names

params.name: object_or_class
params.description: The class name or an object instance

returnDescription: an array of method names defined for the class specified by class_name . In case of an error, it returns null.

name: get_class_vars
description: Get the default properties of the class

params.name: class
params.description: The class name

returnDescription: an associative array of declared properties visible from the current scope, with their default value. The resulting array elements are in the form of varname => value.

name: get_current_user
description: Gets the name of the owner of the current PHP script
returnDescription: the username as a string.

name: get_debug_type
description: Get the type or object name of a variable

params.name: value
params.description: The variable being type checked.

returnDescription: Possibles values for the returned string are: - "int" - "float" - "bool" - "string" - "array" - "null" - A class name for named classes - "class@anonymous" for an anonymous classes - "resource (xxx)" for any resources where "xxx" is a name of resource - "resource (closed)" for closed resources

name: get_declared_classes
description: Returns an array with the name of the defined classes
returnDescription: an array of the names of the declared classes in the current script. Note that depending on what extensions you have compiled or loaded into PHP, additional classes could be present. This means that you will not be able to define your own classes using these names. There is a list of predefined classes in the Predefined Classes section of the appendices.

name: get_declared_interfaces
description: Returns an array of all declared interfaces
returnDescription: an array of the names of the declared interfaces in the current script.

name: get_declared_traits
description: Returns an array of all declared traits
returnDescription: with names of all declared traits in values. Returns NULL in case of a failure.

name: get_defined_constants
description: Returns an associative array with the names of all the constants and their values

params.name: categorize
params.description: [optional] Causing this function to return a multi-dimensional array with categories in the keys of the first dimension and constants and their values in the second dimension. define("MY_CONSTANT", 1); print_r(get_defined_constants(true)); The above example will output something similar to: Array ( [Core] => Array ( [E_ERROR] => 1 [E_WARNING] => 2 [E_PARSE] => 4 [E_NOTICE] => 8 [E_CORE_ERROR] => 16 [E_CORE_WARNING] => 32 [E_COMPILE_ERROR] => 64 [E_COMPILE_WARNING] => 128 [E_USER_ERROR] => 256 [E_USER_WARNING] => 512 [E_USER_NOTICE] => 1024 [E_STRICT] => 2048 [E_RECOVERABLE_ERROR] => 4096 [E_DEPRECATED] => 8192 [E_USER_DEPRECATED] => 16384 [E_ALL] => 32767 [TRUE] => 1 ) [pcre] => Array ( [PREG_PATTERN_ORDER] => 1 [PREG_SET_ORDER] => 2 [PREG_OFFSET_CAPTURE] => 256 [PREG_SPLIT_NO_EMPTY] => 1 [PREG_SPLIT_DELIM_CAPTURE] => 2 [PREG_SPLIT_OFFSET_CAPTURE] => 4 [PREG_GREP_INVERT] => 1 ) [user] => Array ( [MY_CONSTANT] => 1 ) )

name: get_defined_functions
description: Returns an array of all defined functions
returnDescription: an multidimensional array containing a list of all defined functions, both built-in (internal) and user-defined. The internal functions will be accessible via $arr["internal"], and the user defined ones using $arr["user"] (see example below).

name: get_defined_vars
description: Returns an array of all defined variables
returnDescription: A multidimensional array with all the variables.

name: get_error_handler
description: PHP function get_error_handler

name: get_exception_handler
description: PHP function get_exception_handler

description: Returns an array with the names of the functions of a module

params.name: extension
params.description: The module name. This parameter must be in lowercase.

returnDescription: an array with all the functions, or false if module_name is not a valid extension.

name: get_headers
description: Fetches all the headers sent by the server in response to an HTTP request

params.name: url
params.description: The target URL.

returnDescription: an indexed or associative array with the headers, or false on failure.

name: get_html_translation_table
description: Returns the translation table used by htmlspecialchars and htmlentities

params.name: table
params.description: There are two new constants (HTML_ENTITIES, HTML_SPECIALCHARS) that allow you to specify the table you want.

params.name: flags
params.description: [optional] Like the htmlspecialchars and htmlentities functions you can optionally specify the quote_style you are working with. See the description of these modes in htmlspecialchars.

returnDescription: the translation table as an array.

name: get_include_path
description: Gets the current include_path configuration option
returnDescription: the path, as a string.

name: get_included_files
description: Returns an array with the names of included or required files
returnDescription: an array of the names of all files. The script originally called is considered an "included file," so it will be listed together with the files referenced by include and family. Files that are included or required multiple times only show up once in the returned array.

name: get_kind_name
description: PHP function get_kind_name

params.name: kind
params.description: AST_* constant value defining the kind of an AST node

returnDescription: String representation of AST kind value

name: get_loaded_extensions
description: Returns an array with the names of all modules compiled and loaded

params.name: zend_extensions
params.description: [optional] Only return Zend extensions, if not then regular extensions, like mysqli are listed. Defaults to false (return regular extensions).

returnDescription: an indexed array of all the modules names.

name: get_magic_quotes_gpc
description: Gets the current configuration setting of magic quotes gpc
returnDescription: 0 if magic quotes gpc are off, 1 otherwise.

name: get_magic_quotes_runtime
description: Gets the current active configuration setting of magic_quotes_runtime
returnDescription: 0 if magic quotes runtime is off, 1 otherwise.

name: get_mangled_object_vars
description: It returns the same result as (array) $object, with the exception that it ignores overloaded array casts, such as used by ArrayObject.
returnDescription: returns the mangled object properties

name: get_meta_tags
description: Extracts all meta tag content attributes from a file and returns an array

params.name: filename
params.description: The path to the HTML file, as a string. This can be a local file or an URL. What get_meta_tags parses (pay attention to line endings - PHP uses a native function to parse the input, so a Mac file won't work on Unix).

params.name: use_include_path
params.description: [optional] Setting use_include_path to true will result in PHP trying to open the file along the standard include path as per the include_path directive. This is used for local files, not URLs.

returnDescription: an array with all the parsed meta tags. The value of the name property becomes the key, the value of the content property becomes the value of the returned array, so you can easily use standard array functions to traverse it or access single values. Special characters in the value of the name property are substituted with '_', the rest is converted to lower case. If two meta tags have the same name, only the last one is returned.

name: get_metadata
description: Provides metadata for the AST kinds. The returned array is a map from AST kind to a Metadata object.
returnDescription: Metadata about AST kinds

name: get_object_vars
description: Gets the properties of the given object

params.name: object
params.description: An object instance.

returnDescription: an associative array of defined object accessible non-static properties for the specified object in scope. If a property have not been assigned a value, it will be returned with a null value.

name: get_parent_class
description: Retrieves the parent class name for object or class

params.name: object_or_class
params.description: [optional] The tested object or class name

returnDescription: The name of the parent class of the class of which object is an instance or the name. If the object does not have a parent false will be returned. If called without parameter outside object, this function returns false.

name: get_priority_sampling
description: Get the priority sampling level

params.name: global
params.description: If set to 'true' and if there is no active stack (or the active stack doesn't have a root span), then the default priority sampling will be returned, else it will be fetched from the root.

returnDescription: The priority sampling level, or 'null' if an unexpected parameter was given.

name: get_required_files

name: getimagesize
description: Get the size of an image

params.name: filename
params.description: This parameter specifies the file you wish to retrieve information about. It can reference a local file or (configuration permitting) a remote file using one of the supported streams.

returnDescription: an array with 7 elements. Index 0 and 1 contains respectively the width and the height of the image. Some formats may contain no image or may contain multiple images. In these cases, getimagesize might not be able to properly determine the image size. getimagesize will return zero for width and height in these cases. Index 2 is one of the IMAGETYPE_XXX constants indicating the type of the image. Index 3 is a text string with the correct height="yyy" width="xxx" string that can be used directly in an IMG tag. mime is the correspondant MIME type of the image. This information can be used to deliver images with correct the HTTP Content-type header: getimagesize and MIME types channels will be 3 for RGB pictures and 4 for CMYK pictures. bits is the number of bits for each color. For some image types, the presence of channels and bits values can be a bit confusing. As an example, GIF always uses 3 channels per pixel, but the number of bits per pixel cannot be calculated for an animated GIF with a global color table. On failure, false is returned.

name: getimagesizefromstring
description: Get the size of an image from a string.

params.name: string
params.description: The image data, as a string.

returnDescription: Returns an array with 7 elements. Index 0 and 1 contains respectively the width and the height of the image. Index 2 is one of the IMAGETYPE_XXX constants indicating the type of the image. Index 3 is a text string with the correct height="yyy" width="xxx" string that can be used directly in an IMG tag. On failure, FALSE is returned.

name: glob
description: Find pathnames matching a pattern

params.name: pattern
params.description: The pattern. No tilde expansion or parameter substitution is done.

params.name: flags
params.description: Valid flags: GLOB_MARK - Adds a slash to each directory returned GLOB_NOSORT - Return files as they appear in the directory (no sorting). When this flag is not used, the pathnames are sorted alphabetically GLOB_NOCHECK - Return the search pattern if no files matching it were found GLOB_NOESCAPE - Backslashes do not quote metacharacters GLOB_BRACE - Expands {a,b,c} to match 'a', 'b', or 'c' GLOB_ONLYDIR - Return only directory entries which match the pattern GLOB_ERR - Stop on read errors (like unreadable directories), by default errors are ignored.

returnDescription: an array containing the matched files/directories, an empty array if no file matched or false on error. On some systems it is impossible to distinguish between empty match and an error.

name: grapheme_extract
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Function to extract a sequence of default grapheme clusters from a text buffer, which must be encoded in UTF-8.

params.name: haystack
params.description: String to search.

params.name: size
params.description: Maximum number items - based on the $extract_type - to return.

params.name: type
params.description: Defines the type of units referred to by the $size parameter: GRAPHEME_EXTR_COUNT (default) - $size is the number of default grapheme clusters to extract. GRAPHEME_EXTR_MAXBYTES - $size is the maximum number of bytes returned. GRAPHEME_EXTR_MAXCHARS - $size is the maximum number of UTF-8 characters returned.

params.name: offset
params.description: [optional] Starting position in $haystack in bytes - if given, it must be zero or a positive value that is less than or equal to the length of $haystack in bytes. If $start does not point to the first byte of a UTF-8 character, the start position is moved to the next character boundary.

returnDescription: A string starting at offset $start and ending on a default grapheme cluster boundary that conforms to the $size and $extract_type specified.

name: grapheme_levenshtein
description: PHP function grapheme_levenshtein

name: grapheme_str_split
description: PHP function grapheme_str_split

name: grapheme_stripos
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Find position (in grapheme units) of first occurrence of a case-insensitive string

params.name: haystack
params.description: The string to look in. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: needle
params.description: The string to look for. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: offset
params.description: [optional] The optional $offset parameter allows you to specify where in haystack to start searching as an offset in grapheme units (not bytes or characters). The position returned is still relative to the beginning of haystack regardless of the value of $offset.

returnDescription: the position as an integer. If needle is not found, grapheme_stripos() will return boolean FALSE.

name: grapheme_stristr
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Returns part of haystack string from the first occurrence of case-insensitive needle to the end of haystack.

params.name: haystack
params.description: The input string. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: needle
params.description: The string to look for. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: beforeNeedle
params.description: [optional] If TRUE , grapheme_strstr() returns the part of the haystack before the first occurrence of the needle (excluding needle).

returnDescription: the portion of $haystack, or FALSE if $needle is not found.

name: grapheme_strlen
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Get string length in grapheme units

params.name: string
params.description: The string being measured for length. It must be a valid UTF-8 string.

returnDescription: The length of the string on success, and 0 if the string is empty.

name: grapheme_strpos
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Find position (in grapheme units) of first occurrence of a string

params.name: haystack
params.description: The string to look in. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: needle
params.description: The string to look for. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: offset
params.description: [optional] The optional $offset parameter allows you to specify where in $haystack to start searching as an offset in grapheme units (not bytes or characters). The position returned is still relative to the beginning of haystack regardless of the value of $offset.

returnDescription: the position as an integer. If needle is not found, strpos() will return boolean FALSE.

name: grapheme_strripos
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Find position (in grapheme units) of last occurrence of a case-insensitive string

params.name: haystack
params.description: The string to look in. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: needle
params.description: The string to look for. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: offset
params.description: [optional] The optional $offset parameter allows you to specify where in $haystack to start searching as an offset in grapheme units (not bytes or characters). The position returned is still relative to the beginning of haystack regardless of the value of $offset.

returnDescription: the position as an integer. If needle is not found, grapheme_strripos() will return boolean FALSE.

name: grapheme_strrpos
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Find position (in grapheme units) of last occurrence of a string

params.name: haystack
params.description: The string to look in. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: needle
params.description: The string to look for. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: offset
params.description: [optional] The optional $offset parameter allows you to specify where in $haystack to start searching as an offset in grapheme units (not bytes or characters). The position returned is still relative to the beginning of haystack regardless of the value of $offset.

returnDescription: the position as an integer. If needle is not found, grapheme_strrpos() will return boolean FALSE.

name: grapheme_strstr
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Returns part of haystack string from the first occurrence of needle to the end of haystack.

params.name: haystack
params.description: The input string. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: needle
params.description: The string to look for. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: beforeNeedle
params.description: [optional] If TRUE , grapheme_strstr() returns the part of the haystack before the first occurrence of the needle (excluding the needle).

returnDescription: the portion of string, or FALSE if needle is not found.

name: grapheme_substr
description: (PHP 5 >= 5.3.0, PECL intl >= 1.0.0) Return part of a string

params.name: string
params.description: The input string. Must be valid UTF-8.

params.name: offset
params.description: Start position in default grapheme units. If $start is non-negative, the returned string will start at the $start'th position in $string, counting from zero. If $start is negative, the returned string will start at the $start'th grapheme unit from the end of string.

params.name: length
params.description: [optional] Length in grapheme units. If $length is given and is positive, the string returned will contain at most $length grapheme units beginning from $start (depending on the length of string). If $length is given and is negative, then that many grapheme units will be omitted from the end of string (after the start position has been calculated when a start is negative). If $start denotes a position beyond this truncation, FALSE will be returned.

returnDescription: the extracted part of $string, or FALSE if $length is negative and $start denotes a position beyond truncation $length, or also FALSE if $start denotes a position beyond $string length
